title: E7-MT-042-KnowledgeGraphAccessKit

lifecycle.operator_decision_summary: Operator 2026-07-02 ordered per-MT drift spot-checks with adversarial sub-agent review; no MT has been validated by an official validator. Wave-B audit: the NEEDS_MANAGED_RESOURCE_PROOF test ac10_live_pg_round_trip is an unconditional panic! stub - NO real live-test body exists behind the #[ignore] and the integration feature gates nothing here; AC-042-05/06/07/10 + PROOF-042-D DB halves are un-runnable until the test is authored (MT-044/045/046 show the correct pattern). Same live-host orphan as MT-041: install_knowledge_action_registry never called by any host - knowledge.* actions exist only in kittests. Positive: real AccessKit click dispatch with JSON payloads, verified route corrections, malformed-payload no-panic. Fix: author the real live-PG round-trip body (placements row, tag-edge move, edge row SELECTs); shared registry install via the E11 wiring bundle.
lifecycle.remediation_appendage: Author the real live-PG round-trip body (current gated test is an unconditional panic stub); install KnowledgeActionRegistry from the shell.

scope.summary:
GOAL: Expose every interactive action on the native knowledge graph, canvas board, and block-collection views (table/Kanban/calendar) through the WP-011 AccessKit surface with stable author_ids. An out-of-process swarm agent must be able to: navigate the graph to a node by block_id, open a node, add/remove an edge, pan/zoom the canvas, place a block on canvas by id, move a Kanban card between lanes, sort a table column, navigate calendar views — all purely via the AccessKit action channel with no screen-scraping.

EXPECTED DIFF SHAPE:
  CREATE  src/frontend/handshake_native/src/accessibility/knowledge_action_registry.rs
    — defines KnowledgeActionId enum covering graph, canvas, and collection actions
    — defines KnowledgeActionNode struct (same shape as EditorActionNode from MT-041)
    — defines KnowledgeActionRegistry (same Arc<Mutex<>> pattern)
    — graph node AccessKit nodes are keyed by block_id so a swarm agent can target a specific note by its Loom block id
  EDIT    src/frontend/handshake_native/src/graph/graph_pane.rs
    — register graph-level actions: graph.pan-left, graph.pan-right, graph.zoom-in, graph.zoom-out, graph.zoom-reset, graph.open-node (with block_id parameter), graph.add-edge (source_id, target_id), graph.remove-edge (edge_id), graph.select-node, graph.deselect-all
    — register per-node AccessKit nodes keyed 'graph.node.<block_id>': role=TreeItem, actions=['activate' (open), 'expand', 'collapse']; nodes are populated from the in-memory graph projection and updated on graph refresh
  EDIT    src/frontend/handshake_native/src/graph/canvas_pane.rs
    — register canvas-level actions: canvas.pan-left, canvas.pan-right, canvas.zoom-in, canvas.zoom-out, canvas.zoom-reset, canvas.add-card (title parameter), canvas.place-block (block_id, x, y), canvas.remove-placement (placement_id), canvas.add-edge (source_id, target_id, edge_mode), canvas.remove-edge (edge_id), canvas.select-card (placement_id), canvas.deselect-all
    — register per-placement AccessKit nodes keyed 'canvas.card.<placement_id>': role=Group, actions=['activate' (open block), 'move' (x, y), 'delete']
  EDIT    src/frontend/handshake_native/src/graph/collection_pane.rs
    — register collection-level actions: collection.sort (field, direction), collection.filter (field, value), collection.kanban-move (block_id, from_lane, to_lane), collection.calendar-next, collection.calendar-prev, collection.calendar-today, collection.open-block (block_id)
    — register per-row AccessKit nodes keyed 'collection.row.<block_id>': role=Row, actions=['activate']
  EDIT    src/frontend/handshake_native/src/accessibility/mod.rs
    — add pub mod knowledge_action_registry
  CREATE  src/frontend/handshake_native/tests/test_e7_knowledge_accesskit.rs
    — kittest: open graph pane with synthetic blocks, assert per-node AccessKit entries; dispatch graph.open-node, assert navigation; dispatch canvas.place-block, assert new card node; dispatch collection.kanban-move, assert lane change reflected in AccessKit tree

FILES TO CREATE/EDIT (all under allowed paths):
  src/frontend/handshake_native/src/accessibility/knowledge_action_registry.rs  (NEW)
  src/frontend/handshake_native/src/graph/graph_pane.rs                          (EDIT)
  src/frontend/handshake_native/src/graph/canvas_pane.rs                         (EDIT)
  src/frontend/handshake_native/src/graph/collection_pane.rs                     (EDIT)
  src/frontend/handshake_native/src/accessibility/mod.rs                         (EDIT)
  src/frontend/handshake_native/tests/test_e7_knowledge_accesskit.rs            (NEW)

REUSE (existing WP-011 modules — do NOT recreate):
  src/frontend/handshake_native/src/accessibility/*.rs (mod.rs, live.rs, registry.rs, snapshot.rs) — the existing AccessKit adapter + node registry surface; KnowledgeActionRegistry pushes diffs into this same adapter.
  src/frontend/handshake_native/src/loom_graph.rs — existing graph rendering surface; knowledge graph_pane.rs builds on its in-memory graph projection.
  src/frontend/handshake_native/src/canvas_board.rs — existing canvas board surface; knowledge canvas_pane.rs builds on its placement/viewport state.
  src/frontend/handshake_native/src/command_registry.rs + src/frontend/handshake_native/src/event_bus.rs — the shared command/selection/event bus used to dispatch parameterized actions and broadcast selection/refresh events.
  src/frontend/handshake_native/src/mcp/*.rs (tools.rs, action.rs, attribution.rs, session.rs, ...) — the MCP action/tool channel that surfaces AccessKit actions to out-of-process swarm agents.

DEPENDENCIES / UNBLOCK CONDITIONS:
  - WP-KERNEL-011 spike: egui + AccessKit adapter exists and is operational (same dependency as MT-041); the adapter lives in src/frontend/handshake_native/src/accessibility/.
  - MT-021 (local graph view) and MT-022 (global graph view) complete: src/frontend/handshake_native/src/graph/graph_pane.rs exists with a rendered node set and pan/zoom actions.
  - MT-023 (folder tree + tags) complete: tags and tag-hub interaction points exist.
  - MT-025 (wiki-page projection) and MT-026 (canvas board) complete: src/frontend/handshake_native/src/graph/canvas_pane.rs exists with placement management.
  - MT-027 (block-collection views) complete: src/frontend/handshake_native/src/graph/collection_pane.rs exists with table/Kanban/calendar views.

RISK IF MISSED: MT-043 SwarmEditProof requires a swarm agent to 'navigate graph', 'add backlink', and 'drive canvas' purely via AccessKit. If MT-042 is absent those actions have no AccessKit surface and the proof test cannot be written. MT-044 parity gate cannot include knowledge-surface model-accessibility criteria.
acceptance_criteria: AC-042-01: `cargo test -p handshake-native --test test_e7_knowledge_accesskit` passes with zero failures. The test must open graph, canvas, and collection panes with synthetic data (real in-process PG or a seeded test fixture that mirrors the real schema); query the AccessKit tree; and assert per-block and per-placement node presence.
AC-042-02: For each LoomBlock in the test fixture, an AccessKit node with author_id 'graph.node.<block_id>' is present in the tree with role=TreeItem and at least action 'activate'.

AC-042-03: For each canvas placement in the test fixture, an AccessKit node with author_id 'canvas.card.<placement_id>' is present in the tree with role=Group and at least actions 'activate' and 'delete'.

AC-042-04: Dispatching `graph.open-node` with a block_id parameter via AccessKit causes the corresponding document to open in the editor pane (observable as a new AccessKit node for the opened document within 200 ms).

AC-042-05: Dispatching `canvas.place-block` with a valid block_id, x=100, y=100 causes a call to the backend `place_block_on_canvas` API and a new 'canvas.card.<new_placement_id>' node to appear in the AccessKit tree after the next refresh.

AC-042-06: Dispatching `collection.kanban-move` with a block_id, from_lane, and to_lane causes a call to the backend `update_loom_block` API (tag edge update) and the collection AccessKit tree to reflect the block in the new lane on next query.
AC-042-07: Dispatching `graph.add-edge` with source_id and target_id causes a call to the backend `create_loom_edge` API and a new edge node to appear in the graph AccessKit tree.

AC-042-08: All graph-level action nodes (graph.pan-left through graph.zoom-reset) are present in the AccessKit tree regardless of whether any blocks are loaded; they represent global graph controls, not per-node actions.
AC-042-09: `cargo build -p handshake-native` succeeds with zero new warnings introduced by this MT.
AC-042-10: No mocks substitute backend API calls in the proof tests; the test must connect to a real in-process or test-fixture PostgreSQL instance and assert round-trip database state.
proof_targets: PROOF-042-A: Run `cargo test -p handshake-native --test test_e7_knowledge_accesskit 2>&1`; paste full stdout. Every line must show 'ok'.

PROOF-042-B: In the kittest, print the AccessKit tree and paste it. Reviewer must be able to locate at least two 'graph.node.<uuid>' nodes, one 'canvas.card.<uuid>' node, and all graph-level action nodes.
PROOF-042-C: After dispatching `canvas.place-block`, print the AccessKit tree again and paste the diff showing the new 'canvas.card.<id>' node.
PROOF-042-D: After dispatching `collection.kanban-move`, query the backend (SELECT tag_edges WHERE block_id=...) and paste the row showing the tag has moved to the new lane.
PROOF-042-E: Run `cargo build -p handshake-native 2>&1 | grep -E 'warning:|error:'`; paste output. Zero new warnings.
implementation_notes: IN-042-01: Graph node AccessKit registration. The native graph pane maintains a Vec<GraphNode> (each GraphNode has block_id: Uuid, title: String, position: Vec2, expanded: bool). On every frame where the node set has changed (content hash differs), update the KnowledgeActionRegistry with the current node set. Each node registers as AccessKit node author_id='graph.node.<block_id>' with role=TreeItem. Do NOT re-register unchanged nodes each frame; only push diffs. The registry must keep a BTreeMap<Uuid, u64> of per-node content hashes to detect changes cheaply.

IN-042-02: Canvas placement AccessKit registration. The native canvas pane maintains a Vec<CanvasPlacement> (placement_id: Uuid, placed_block_id: Uuid, x, y, w, h). Register each placement as author_id='canvas.card.<placement_id>'. The placed_block_id is surfaced as an AccessKit property (key: 'block_id', value: placed_block_id string) so a swarm agent can correlate placements to their source blocks. Role=Group. Actions=['activate' (opens the referenced block), 'move' (requires x, y parameters passed via AccessKit custom-action payload), 'delete'].

IN-042-03: Collection row AccessKit registration. The native collection pane (table/Kanban/calendar) maintains a Vec<CollectionRow> (block_id, fields). Register each row as author_id='collection.row.<block_id>' with role=Row. For Kanban, additionally register lane containers: author_id='collection.lane.<lane_tag>' with role=Group so a swarm agent can assert which lane a card is in.

IN-042-04: Parameterized actions. AccessKit's custom-action payload is a string. Encode parameters as JSON in the payload string. Example: `canvas.place-block` receives payload `{"block_id":"<uuid>","x":100,"y":100}`. The canvas pane's action handler parses this JSON and calls `place_block_on_canvas`. Use `serde_json::from_str` for parsing; log and discard malformed payloads with an error log line (never panic in release builds).

IN-042-05: React parity references for this MT (READ ONLY):
  app/src/components/LoomCanvasBoard.tsx — canvas drag/drop, place-block, add-card, add-edge, viewport persistence (pan/zoom), createCanvasCard, placeBlockOnCanvas, addCanvasVisualEdge, createLoomEdge, updateCanvasBoardViewport
  app/src/components/BlockCollectionView.tsx — queryBlockViewResults, updateBlockView (sort), updateLoomBlock (Kanban move), flipDirection, BLOCK_VIEW_UNTAGGED_LANE
  app/src/components/LoomSearchV2Panel.tsx — loomSearchV2, createBlockView, content_type_facets, onOpenBlock (reference semantics)
  app/src/components/LoomWikiPagePanel.tsx — getLoomWikiProjection (for wiki-page node in graph)
  app/src/lib/api.ts — backend function signatures: getCanvasBoard, placeBlockOnCanvas, createCanvasCard, createLoomEdge, addCanvasVisualEdge, removeCanvasPlacement, updateCanvasBoardViewport, getBlockView, queryBlockViewResults, updateBlockView, updateLoomBlock, loomSearchV2, createBlockView, getLoomBlock

IN-042-06: Backend API bindings. All backend calls for this MT go through the existing E6 cluster wiring (MT-037 to MT-040). Do NOT add new IPC Tauri commands in this MT. If a required API is not yet wired in E6, file a typed blocker against the relevant E6 MT and skip that AC with a TODO comment citing the blocker.

IN-042-07: Graph pan/zoom AccessKit nodes. Register global control nodes regardless of graph content:
  graph.pan-left      — Button, action='activate'
  graph.pan-right     — Button
  graph.zoom-in       — Button
  graph.zoom-out      — Button
  graph.zoom-reset    — Button
  graph.select-node   — requires block_id parameter in payload
  graph.deselect-all  — Button
For graph.add-edge and graph.remove-edge: receive JSON payload {"source_id":"...","target_id":"..."} and {"edge_id":"..."} respectively.

IN-042-08: KnowledgeActionRegistry is a separate struct from EditorActionRegistry (MT-041) but shares the same AccessKit adapter reference (the WP-011 adapter under src/frontend/handshake_native/src/accessibility/). Both registries push diffs to the same AccessKit adapter. Ensure no duplicate author_ids across both registries; the 'graph.', 'canvas.', 'collection.' prefixes disambiguate from the 'editor.' prefix in MT-041.

IN-042-09: kittest fixture. The kittest must seed at least: 3 LoomBlocks (one of type 'note', one of type 'canvas', one of type 'view_def'), 2 Loom edges between them, 2 canvas placements on the canvas block, and 2 collection rows with one Kanban lane each. Use the same real-PG test fixture pattern established in the E6 cluster (MT-037/MT-038) if available; otherwise use a local in-process fixture that directly populates the in-memory graph projection structs (acceptable only if real-PG fixture is not yet available in the test harness). PostgreSQL/EventLedger only — no SQLite.

IN-042-10: HBR-SWARM author_id stability for dynamic collections. Graph and canvas nodes come and go as blocks are created/deleted. A swarm agent's stored 'graph.node.<block_id>' reference is valid as long as the block exists. Document this contract in a Rust doc comment on KnowledgeActionRegistry: 'graph.node.<block_id> is stable for the lifetime of the block; canvas.card.<placement_id> is stable for the lifetime of the placement. Deletion is signalled by absence from the AccessKit tree, not by a tombstone.'

IN-042-11: Cross-WP dependency. This MT depends on the WP-KERNEL-011 egui + AccessKit adapter spike (originally tracked as WP-KERNEL-011 MT-001). The adapter and AccessKit node registry it produced live under src/frontend/handshake_native/src/accessibility/ (mod.rs, live.rs, registry.rs, snapshot.rs) and must be operational before this MT can be implemented. This is a cross-work-packet prerequisite and is recorded here as prose rather than in depends_on, which lists only in-WP (WP-KERNEL-012) microtasks.

PATH CORRECTION - the contract's graph pane filenames are WRONG (KERNEL_BUILDER gate 2026-06-23, verified via Get-ChildItem): graph/graph_pane.rs / canvas_pane.rs / collection_pane.rs DO NOT EXIST. The REAL graph/ files: graph/graph_view.rs (MT-021 graph view), graph/canvas_board.rs (MT-026 Loom canvas with placements - NOT the root WP-011 canvas_board.rs), graph/block_collection_view.rs (MT-027 table/Kanban/calendar). allowed_paths corrected. knowledge_action_registry.rs goes in accessibility/ (root) + register via accessibility/mod.rs - same as MT-041's EditorActionRegistry.

REUSE EXISTING author_ids - CONSOLIDATE like MT-041 (KERNEL_BUILDER gate): MT-027 ALREADY emits bcv.table.*/bcv.kanban.*/bcv.calendar.*/bcv.kind.* author_ids for the collection; MT-026 emits canvas placement node author_ids (with loom:// chips MT-032); MT-021 emits graph node author_ids; MT-024 sidebar. MT-042 CONSOLIDATES into KnowledgeActionRegistry under the canonical graph.*/canvas.*/collection.* convention + makes them DISPATCHABLE (AccessKit custom-action channel) + adds per-identity keyed nodes (graph.node.<block_id> TreeItem, canvas.card.<placement_id> Group, collection.row.<block_id> Row, collection.lane.<tag> Group). ALIAS the existing widget handlers - do NOT re-mint a 2nd parallel node for a widget that already has an author_id (the MT-041 discipline). The 'graph.'/'canvas.'/'collection.' prefixes disambiguate from MT-041's 'editor.' - assert no cross-registry author_id collision (IN-042-08). NO-MOCK-NODES: every node = a REAL rendered widget/placement/row (the MT-041 AC-08 anti-scaffolding gate); a node for a non-drawn widget is a FAILURE.

BACKEND VIA E6 + REAL-PG GATED + PARSE SAFETY (KERNEL_BUILDER gate): all backend calls (place-block, kanban-move, add-edge, sort, viewport) route through the E6 loom client (MT-038 backend/loom.rs - the VERIFIED routes: canvas placements POST /loom/canvas/{id}/placements MT-026; kanban-move = updateLoomBlock add_tags/remove_tags MT-027; create_loom_edge; updateBlockView sort) - NOT new IPC (IN-042-06). REAL-PG REALITY (resolve AC-042-10 vs no-live-backend): the AccessKit registration + dispatch + the E6 request-SHAPE (right route/body built) are provable STANDALONE with an in-memory graph-projection fixture (IN-042-09 permits this); the DB ROUND-TRIP assertions (place-block->loom_canvas_placements row, kanban-move->tag edge, add-edge->edge row, AC-042-05/06/07/10 + PROOF-D) are NEEDS_MANAGED_RESOURCE_PROOF (gated #[ignore], NOT faked, NOT a fake-PG). Parameterized action payload = JSON; parse via serde_json::from_str MATCH (NEVER unwrap - RISK-03/CTRL-03 no panic on malformed; add a malformed-payload no-panic test). placement_id = real UUID v4 (RISK-02/CTRL-02).

CHURN/VIEWPORT/QUIET + HYGIENE (KERNEL_BUILDER gate): separate VIEWPORT state (pan/zoom) from the NODE SET - only re-register graph/canvas AccessKit nodes when the node set CHANGES (per-node content-hash BTreeMap), NOT every pan/zoom frame (RISK-01/CTRL-01 + RISK-06/CTRL-06: viewport+50-lookahead pagination for large graphs; document the off-screen-needs-pan contract). push-on-change diffs only, hash-check before notify (HBR-QUIET). graph.pan-*/zoom-* registered ONCE as fixed global controls regardless of content (AC-042-08). per-identity nodes keyed by block_id/placement_id (HBR-SWARM stable; deletion = absence not tombstone, IN-042-10). NO Color32 literal (theme guard). AccessKit tree dump = HBR-VIS proof printed to stdout (no screenshot needed). Full suite stays green (touches graph_view/canvas_board/block_collection_view - shared MT-021/026/027 files; don't regress their tests). accesskit 0.21.1 field-correct roles (TreeItem/Group/Row/Button).
ports_from_react: app/src/components/LoomCanvasBoard.tsx — canvas board state (pan/zoom/placements/edges), drag/drop, viewport persistence; port the full action surface to AccessKit nodes and parameterized actions. Native reuse target: src/frontend/handshake_native/src/canvas_board.rs.
app/src/components/BlockCollectionView.tsx — collection views (table/Kanban/calendar), sort persistence, Kanban card move (real tag edge update); port to collection AccessKit nodes

app/src/components/LoomSearchV2Panel.tsx — search action surface, facet navigation, save-as-view; port to graph/collection AccessKit nodes. Native reuse target: src/frontend/handshake_native/src/search_rail.rs.
app/src/components/LoomWikiPagePanel.tsx — wiki-page projection action; port to graph node open-node action. Native reuse target: src/frontend/handshake_native/src/loom_graph.rs.
app/src/components/HsLinkView.tsx — Loom block preview on hover; the native equivalent should surface a 'preview' action on graph.node.<block_id> AccessKit nodes
binds_backend_api: handshake_core: GET /loom/canvas/{board_block_id} (getCanvasBoard) — read canvas state for AccessKit tree population
handshake_core: POST /loom/canvas/{board_block_id}/place (placeBlockOnCanvas) — triggered by canvas.place-block action
handshake_core: POST /loom/canvas/{board_block_id}/card (createCanvasCard) — triggered by canvas.add-card action
handshake_core: POST /loom/edges (createLoomEdge) — triggered by graph.add-edge (semantic edge)
handshake_core: POST /loom/canvas/{board_block_id}/visual-edge (addCanvasVisualEdge) — triggered by canvas.add-edge with edge_mode=visual
handshake_core: DELETE /loom/canvas/{board_block_id}/placement/{placement_id} (removeCanvasPlacement) — triggered by canvas.card.<id> delete action
handshake_core: PUT /loom/canvas/{board_block_id}/viewport (updateCanvasBoardViewport) — triggered by pan/zoom actions
handshake_core: GET /loom/views/{view_block_id} (getBlockView) + GET /loom/views/{view_block_id}/results (queryBlockViewResults) — read collection for AccessKit tree
handshake_core: PUT /loom/views/{view_block_id} (updateBlockView) — triggered by collection.sort action
handshake_core: PUT /loom/blocks/{block_id} (updateLoomBlock) — triggered by collection.kanban-move action (tag edge update)

hbr_obligations: HBR-INT: kittest in src/frontend/handshake_native/tests/test_e7_knowledge_accesskit.rs must pass in CI; all AC must be green before MT is DONE

HBR-SWARM: per-block and per-placement AccessKit nodes must use block_id/placement_id as the stable id segment so swarm agents can reference nodes by Loom identity; author_ids must not include positional or frame-count segments
HBR-VIS: kittest must print the AccessKit tree to stdout; graph node list and canvas card list must be human-readable in the output
HBR-QUIET: graph and canvas AccessKit updates must only push diffs when the node set has actually changed; hash-check the node set before calling notify_accesskit_adapter

risks: RISK-042-01: Graph node churn — if the graph refreshes on every pan/zoom frame (e.g. due to a viewport-dependent LOD filter), the per-node AccessKit node set is re-registered every frame, causing excessive diff traffic and potentially breaking swarm agent node references mid-operation.

RISK-042-02: Canvas placement_id instability — if placement_ids are generated as sequential integers rather than stable UUIDs, a delete+add cycle reuses old integers and a swarm agent's stored reference points to the wrong placement.
RISK-042-03: Parameterized action payload injection — a malformed or malicious JSON payload in a custom action dispatch could cause a panic or unwrap in the action handler, crashing the UI thread.

RISK-042-04: Collection Kanban move race — if two swarm agents simultaneously dispatch kanban-move for the same block to different lanes, the last write wins with no conflict detection, silently dropping one move.

RISK-042-05: Backend not wired (E6 blocker) — the canvas.place-block action dispatches to placeBlockOnCanvas, but if MT-037/MT-038 E6 wiring is not complete, the action silently does nothing and the kittest passes only because the test does not assert backend state.

RISK-042-06: AccessKit tree size — a workspace with 5000+ Loom blocks causes 5000+ AccessKit nodes in the graph pane, which may overflow AccessKit's tree serialization budget or make the kittest slow.
minimum_controls: CTRL-042-01: Separate viewport state (pan/zoom) from the graph node set. Only re-register AccessKit nodes when the node set changes (block add/remove/rename), not on every pan/zoom frame. Pan/zoom actions are registered once as fixed global actions, not per-frame.

CTRL-042-02: placement_ids must be UUIDs (Uuid::new_v4()) generated at creation time and persisted in the backend. Assert in the kittest that placement_ids are 36-character UUID strings and are stable across a refresh cycle.

CTRL-042-03: All custom-action payload parsing must use a match on serde_json::from_str result; never use unwrap(). Log the error and early-return on parse failure. Add a kittest case that dispatches a malformed payload and asserts no panic.

CTRL-042-04: The kanban-move implementation must use an optimistic-lock or last-write-wins with EventLedger sequencing from the existing backend. Do NOT add a new conflict resolution mechanism in this MT; rely on the existing EventLedger ordering. Document the behavior in a code comment.

CTRL-042-05: In the kittest, after dispatching canvas.place-block, assert the database row exists (SELECT from loom_canvas_placements WHERE placement_id=...) before asserting the AccessKit tree. If the backend call failed, the test must fail with the API error, not with a missing-node assertion.

CTRL-042-06: Graph AccessKit registration must support pagination: only register AccessKit nodes for blocks currently visible in the graph viewport plus a 50-node lookahead buffer. Blocks outside the buffer are unregistered and re-registered on scroll. Document this contract on KnowledgeActionRegistry. Swarm agents that need to access an off-screen node must first dispatch graph.pan or graph.select-node to bring it into the viewport.

research_provenance.source: KERNEL_BUILDER gate + real graph/ tree (graph_view/canvas_board/block_collection_view) + MT-021/024/026/027 existing author_ids + MT-038 E6 loom client + MT-041 registry pattern + MT-035 anti-scaffolding
corrections: PATH FIX: graph_pane/canvas_pane/collection_pane fictional -> graph_view.rs/canvas_board.rs/block_collection_view.rs; registry in accessibility/ root
reuse/consolidate MT-021/026/027 author_ids (bcv.* etc.) - alias, don't re-mint; no cross-registry collision (graph./canvas./collection. vs editor.); no-mock-nodes

backend via E6 loom client (MT-038 verified routes: /loom/canvas placements, updateLoomBlock tags kanban, create_loom_edge); DB round-trip NEEDS_MANAGED_RESOURCE_PROOF, request-shape+registration standalone
payload serde match no-unwrap (no panic); UUID placement_ids; viewport separate from node-set + hash-check (HBR-QUIET); pan/zoom global once; per-identity stable nodes; full suite green

implementation_result.implemented_by: KERNEL_BUILDER folded pipeline wf_ff042ee3 (impl + 3-lens panel + harden)
commits: 184bd480 feat MT-042 declare + route canvas card delete custom action
implementation_result.proofs: AC-042-01..09 PASS, AC-042-10 + PROOF-D = NEEDS_MANAGED_RESOURCE_PROOF (honest gate); 8 unit + 11 kittest (+1 ignored) + regression (canvas 18, graph_view 6, bcv 23, MT-041 editor 13, accesskit_ids 9, mcp_tools 10, swarm_concurrency 7) green; 0 warnings; no artifacts; 54-node knowledge.* AccessKit tree dumped (HBR-VIS, no screenshot).
implementation_result.path_fix_held: PATH correction held: built in graph/graph_view.rs (MT-021) + graph/canvas_board.rs (MT-026) + graph/block_collection_view.rs (MT-027) - the contract's graph_pane/canvas_pane/collection_pane were fictional (KERNEL_BUILDER-verified absent before launch).
implementation_result.reuse_held: KnowledgeActionRegistry consolidates under graph.*/canvas.*/collection.*; graph.node. reuses MT-021's NODE_AUTHOR_ID_PREFIX (alias not re-mint); collection reuses MT-027's card_move_tags helper; no cross-surface author_id collision with MT-041's editor.* (IN-042-08). Per-identity keyed nodes: graph.node.<block_id> TreeItem, canvas.card.<placement_id> Group (block_id value), collection.row.<block_id> Row, collection.lane.<tag> Group.
implementation_result.dispatch_and_backend: dispatch reaches the panes (graph.open-node->OpenNode, canvas.place-block->PlaceBlock+new card, card delete->RemovePlacement, kanban-move->CardMove add/remove-tags shape, graph.add-edge->AddEdge); backend via the E6 loom-client request SHAPE (MT-038 verified routes: /loom/canvas placements MT-026, updateLoomBlock tags MT-027, createLoomEdge).
